question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts requests with no filters, causing NftService.getNfts() to execute full-table scans that exhaust database CPU and connection pool resources, degrading or crashing 30%+ of mirror node processing instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with simultaneous token.id lower bound (gte:X), token.id upper bound (lte:Y), serialnumber lower bound (gte:A), and serialnumber upper bound (lte:B) to force getLowerFilters(), getInnerFilters(), and getUpperFilters() to generate a three-part UNION query, causing disproportionate database load that degrades 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user flood /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with many concurrent requests using valid account IDs, causing EntityService.isValidAccount() to issue repeated entityExistenceQuery SELECT statements that saturate the database connection pool and degrade 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards request with the maximum allowed number of repeated timestamp=eq: parameters, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a large IN-clause that generates an expensive database query, and when repeated concurrently, exhausts DB CPU across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a long base32 alias string as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to invoke getAccountIdFromAlias() which executes entityFromAliasQuery against the entity table, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a 40-character hex EVM address as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() which executes entityFromEvmAddressQuery with a Buffer.from() hex decode, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database connection pool resources across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with multiple spender.id=eq: values up to the maxRepeatedQueryParameters limit, causing spenderIdInFilters to accumulate many entries that are passed to NftService.getNfts() as a large IN-clause, generating expensive queries that when issued concurrently degrade 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue concurrent requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with limit set to the maximum allowed value (100), causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to fetch and return large result sets that exhaust Node.js heap memory across 30%+ of processing instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with both timestamp=gte:X and timestamp=lte:Y spanning the entire history of the chain, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to scan the full staking_reward_transfer table, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly follow the pagination next link returned by getPaginationLink() in rapid succession, causing a sustained sequence of NftService.getNfts() queries that cumulatively exhaust database resources and degrade 30%+ of mirror node processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with token.id=gte:0 and serialnumber=gte:0 to force extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to produce a lower-bound multi-union query via getLowerFilters() and getInnerFilters(), generating a more expensive SQL UNION that when issued at scale degrades 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid account IDs and issue concurrent /accounts/{id}/rewards requests with maximum limit, causing both EntityService.isValidAccount() and StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to execute back-to-back database queries per request, doubling the DB load and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with limit=100 (max) and no token.id or serialnumber filters, causing NftService.getNfts() to execute an unfiltered query against the nft table returning the maximum row count, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database memory and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit the maximum number of distinct timestamp=eq: values allowed by maxRepeatedQueryParameters, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a large IN-clause appended to the staking_reward_transfer query, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database CPU across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.evmAddress path parameter (e.g., 0.0.<40-hex-chars>) to force EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() with a DB lookup, and when issued at high concurrency with many distinct EVM addresses, exhaust database connection slots and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that pass validateFilters() with a single spender.id=lte:X and a single spender.id=gte:Y (both allowed), causing the resulting multi-condition NFT query to perform a range scan on the spender column that is more expensive than a point lookup, and when issued at scale, degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards using a base32 alias as the path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getAccountIdFromAlias() (DB lookup) followed by EntityService.isValidAccount() (second DB lookup), doubling per-request DB queries and degrading 30%+ of nodes at scale?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with token.id=lte:X and serialnumber=lte:Y to force getUpperFilters() to generate an upper-bound sub-query, combined with getLowerFilters() and getInnerFilters() producing a three-part UNION, causing disproportionate database load that degrades 30%+ of nodes when issued concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with order=asc and no other filters, causing NftService.getNfts() to perform a full ascending scan of the nft table for the given account, and when issued at high concurrency across many account IDs, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly follow the pagination next link (generated when response.rewards.length === query.limit) to issue a long chain of sequential /accounts/{id}/rewards requests, causing sustained database load that cumulatively degrades 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with token.id=eq:X and serialnumber=eq:Y, causing getLowerFilters() to include all four bound filters (primary.equal, primary.lower, primary.upper, secondary.equal) in a single query, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database resources and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a non-existent EVM address as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() which performs a full DB lookup returning no rows, and when issued at high concurrency with many distinct non-existent addresses, exhaust database connection pool and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with timestamp=gte:0 (epoch start) and limit=100, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to scan the entire staking_reward_transfer table from the beginning, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with spender.id=gte:0 (matching all spenders) combined with limit=100, causing NftService.getNfts() to scan the maximum number of NFT rows with a broad spender range filter, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue concurrent requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards using EVM addresses as path parameters, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() (DB lookup) followed by isValidAccount() (second DB lookup) per request, and when issued at scale, exhaust database connections and degrade 30%+ of nodes?
